retrieved.text: - Changing your teaching subjects also constitutes a change of degree programme. This means, from winter semester 2026/27 onwards, changing your teaching subject will automatically result in being transferred to the new curriculum. You do not need to apply separately for the transfer. - If you study in combination with another university, you also have to notify them of any changes. [Information about the new curriculum](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/umstieg-in-das-neue-lehramtsstudium) (only in German) #### Transcultural Communication: changing the amount of working languages It is possible to change the number of working languages in the bachelor programme Transcultural Communication once. This means that you can switch from studying with two working languages (A, Bx) and extension curricula to studying with three working languages (A, Bx and By) or vice versa only once. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the admission period (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). Specify the number of working languages and which languages (A, Bx and if applicable By) you choose. The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text:
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement

  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a completed degree programme,
  - with English language specific courses,
  - to the extent of at least 36 ECTS.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 115 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 or higher
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), especially §§ 63, 64](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- Decree: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/02_pdf/20250205-13.pdf) (05.02.2025)
- For applications for admission until winter semester 2024/25 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2021_2022/2021_2022_30.pdf) (17.12.2021)

retrieved.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
retrieved.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

retrieved.title: Bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

reranked.text: - Changing your teaching subjects also constitutes a change of degree programme. This means, from winter semester 2026/27 onwards, changing your teaching subject will automatically result in being transferred to the new curriculum. You do not need to apply separately for the transfer. - If you study in combination with another university, you also have to notify them of any changes. [Information about the new curriculum](https://lehramt.univie.ac.at/umstieg-in-das-neue-lehramtsstudium) (only in German) #### Transcultural Communication: changing the amount of working languages It is possible to change the number of working languages in the bachelor programme Transcultural Communication once. This means that you can switch from studying with two working languages (A, Bx) and extension curricula to studying with three working languages (A, Bx and By) or vice versa only once. Send us your message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) during the admission period (in this case it does not make a difference where your secondary-school leaving certificate is from). Specify the number of working languages and which languages (A, Bx and if applicable By) you choose. The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. reranked.title: Proof of English language proficiency
reranked.text:
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement

  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a completed degree programme,
  - with English language specific courses,
  - to the extent of at least 36 ECTS.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 115 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 or higher
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), especially §§ 63, 64](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- Decree: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/02_pdf/20250205-13.pdf) (05.02.2025)
- For applications for admission until winter semester 2024/25 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2021_2022/2021_2022_30.pdf) (17.12.2021)

reranked.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
reranked.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

reranked.title: Bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).
